assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row reads: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3: 2 failing assertions (11/13 passed, 85% pass rate); eval-1: 100%, eval-2: 100%, eval-4: 100%, eval-5: 100%; overall: 91% pass rate. 1 sub-task created for eval-3 regression failures. |'. The verdict is WARN, and the details confirm Eval Quality is WARN due to eval-3 having 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row contains detailed eval metrics: 'eval-3: 2 failing assertions (11/13 passed, 85% pass rate); eval-1: 100%, eval-2: 100%, eval-4: 100%, eval-5: 100%; overall: 91% pass rate'. These per-eval pass rates and failing assertion counts confirm the orchestrator detected and processed an eval result review, extracting structured metrics from it.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: In review-50001.md, the comment from reviewer-b is classified as 'Suggestion' with reasoning about reviewer language ('Consider adding'), nature of feedback, task scope, and convention backing. There is no mention of eval detection, eval metrics, pass rates, or assertion counts. The file processes it as a normal review comment classification. The report.md row 'Review Feedback | PASS | 1 review comment classified as suggestion; no code change requests' also treats it as a normal review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row includes: 'eval-3: 2 failing assertions (11/13 passed, 85% pass rate)' providing the pass rate and failing assertion count. The report's summary paragraph further specifies: 'Eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002.' Together, the Test Quality row provides the metrics and the report includes the specific failing assertion subjects (convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation).

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and its Description reads: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002. Two assertions fail because the verify-pr skill does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility for suggestion-classified comments...' The Review Context section includes both failing assertions with their full text and evidence verbatim.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: In subtask-1.md, the '## Review Context' section includes: Failing Assertion 1 with assertion text 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' and evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented...'; Failing Assertion 2 with assertion text 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' and evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted...'

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence:
In subtask-1.md, the last section reads: '## Target PR
https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the table row reads: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | investigation ran on eval failure sub-task for eval-3 convention upgrade eligibility |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A), confirming the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), subtask-1.md (sub-task description), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). All output files are verification artifacts (reports, classifications, sub-task descriptions, criterion evaluations). No code files appear in the outputs, and the report shows Scope Containment: PASS confirming changes were limited to the PR's existing diff.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md: 'Scope Containment | PASS | changes limited to style-conventions.md and SKILL.md as specified in Files to Modify' evaluates diff files against task scope independently. 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | no secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in diff' scans the diff for secrets independently. 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met' and the 7 criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) each evaluate specific acceptance criteria against the PR diff content (Check 6 symbol scanning, documentation verification, verdict logic, output format, verdict mapping) with no reference to eval results in their reasoning.
